user_symptom: Customers will experience increased latency. Query (and other) jobs will timeout, or fail.
Affected customers experienced elevated error rates and latency when connecting to Google APIs.

Google Cloud Console - Affected customers in Europe were unable to load the console, or experienced timeouts.
Container Registry - Affected customers were unable to connect to the service using the API in europe-west1.
Cloud Bigtable - Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations.
Cloud Spanner - Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations.
Cloud Endpoints - Service was unavailable in europe-west1 and europe-west4.
Firebase Messaging - Affected customers experienced issues receiving notifications in asia-southeast1.
Cloud SQL - Affected customers experienced probe creation request failures in europe-west1, europe-west4, and europe-west5.
Cloud Eventarc - Affected customers were unable to create triggers in europe-west1.
App Engine - Affected customers experienced elevated errors in europe-west1.
Cloud Spanner experienced elevated latency spikes which may have resulted in connection timeouts.

Customers may have experienced errors or incomplete monitoring data.
-Missing precomputed data from between 11:00 PT and 12:45 PT is expected, but can still be viewed via raw query.
-Customers may have also experienced false alerts during the impact window based on the underlying monitoring data.
Cloud Networking traffic going through us-central1 and us-east1 shows increased latency.
APIs, Credentials, Monitoring, IAM and several other pages on the Cloud console are loading slowly and may ended up as a loading failure.
UNKNOWN
customers experiencing elevated rates of packet loss
Slow or stuck operations for zonal PD-Standard devices in us-west1-b

* Container Registry - Affected customers were unable to connect to the service using the API in europe-west1.
* Cloud Bigtable - Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations.
* Cloud Spanner - Affected customers in Europe experienced unavailable errors and latency for both data and admin operations.
* Cloud Endpoints - Service was unavailable in europe-west1 and europe-west4.
* Firebase Messaging - Affected customers experienced issues receiving notifications in asia-southeast1.
* Cloud SQL - Affected customers experienced probe creation request failures in europe-west1, europe-west4, and europe-west5.
* Cloud Eventarc - Affected customers were unable to create triggers in europe-west1.
App Engine - Affected customers experienced elevated errors in europe-west1.

service_name: Multiple Products including Google Workspace and Google Cloud Platform
user_symptom: Affected customers encountered error 500 when accessing calendar.google.com; Customers experienced elevated API traffic error rates; Customers may have experienced generic unavailable errors; Customers may have noticed generic status unavailable errors; Customers may have encountered error messages that state Policy Checks are unavailable; Customers may have experienced elevated latency, operation deadline expirations and/or error messages that state Policy Checks are unavailable.
Significant packet loss on Cloud Interconnect in europe-west2 for some customers.
Queries exhibited higher than normal latency. - Customers were unable to fully utilize their reserved slots.
Affected customers may have experienced delayed log entries in Log Explorer or other export destinations for the duration of the incident.

Affected Customers may have observed issues with network partitions and accessing workload VMs.
- Affected Customers may have experienced connectivity issues using vCenter, HCX, NSX Manager, and NSX-T Edge virtual machines.
Logs-based metrics data points may be missing or delayed
Cloud logs might be unavailable for 25% of customers in global location. Logs won't be available, high latency when reading data.
Issues with seeing monitoring metric data especially delivery_latency_health_score. Other metrics data may also be affected.

Customers using CNAME chasing may experience an issue with Cloud DNS. Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer.
Cloud Pub/Sub publish, pull and ack methods experienced elevated latency, leading to timeouts. The average error rate for the issue duration was 0.66%.
Increased TLS handshake errors or connection terminations for GCLB traffic served from HTTPS load balancers in Western Europe; HTTP load balancing returning error for some user in Europe.
Users experiencing failure with file uploads
Customers may not be able to look at live logs by running "tail" commands

Some customers using Cloud Networking experienced severe traffic disruption for the two occurrences of the incident.
Some cloud customers communicating outside the affected regions (including to the Internet) would have seen two periods of disruption, ~8 minutes at 04:30 AM, ~10 minutes at 04:48 AM US/Pacific.
GKE cluster pods restart when docker restarts.
customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d
preventing customers from creating or updating Support cases. Cloud Console pages that are nested under https://console.cloud.google.com/support may have also failed to load or update.
Attempts to create a new instance or to delete an instance fail with an error.
This may appear as timeouts, 5XX errors and potential authorization errors being returned from the service.
Customers may see containers restarting when docker daemon is restarted. Clusters may see pods restarting when Dockers are restarted.
Customers impacted by this issue would have experienced abnormal instance terminations for instances which were running in the impacted building.

Cloud SQL External Server or Database Migration Service (DMS) users who want to migrate to Cloud SQL MySQL 5.7 with GTID replication may experience data inconsistency (i.e. data in Cloud SQL may not be consistent with data on the source) issue if they have insert/update/delete transactions during the dump phase. Further more, if customer has updates to those inconsistent data during replication phase, the migration job may fail.

Google Cloud Networking experienced increased latency, packet loss, and service unavailable errors for traffic between regions and from Google to external endpoints including on-premises environments and the internet.
Connectivity issues in us-central1 resulting in failures and elevated latency.
users attempting to update or deploy Cloud Load Balancers may experience delays or failures.

Customers using CNAME chasing may experience an issue with Cloud DNS. Querying for a record defined in one private zone that points to a record in another private zone does not return the final answer.

customers impacted by this incident would likely experience a total unavailability of zonal services hosted in us-east1-c or us-east1-d. It is possible for customers to experience service interruption in none, one, or both zones.
Customers are unable to login to their admin workstation in both their prod and non-prod environments.

Customers attempting to create github triggers using a Cloud Console URL that ends in their project id will see a "Failed to load" error with following message:
"There was an error while loading /cloud-build/triggers?project=
Please try again"

Some customers might experience a connection failure in Docker workflow to GCLB, GCB, or Fastly destinations such as debian.org, github.com with a timeout error.
Users might experience higher latency.
errors with creating and deleting Cloud Composer environments
resulting in network packet loss

Allocation of new external IP addresses for GCE instance creation continues to be unavailable in us-east4. The Google Compute Engine service is experiencing delays in network programming in us-east1.

Users may see slow performance or intermittent errors.

Google Cloud Storage started experiencing degraded availability for some objects based in us-central1 buckets (Regional, Nearline, Coldline, Durable Reduced Availability) and US multi-region buckets. Between 08:00 and 18:00 PST error rate was ~3.5%, error rates have since decreased to 0.5%. Errors are expected to be consistent for objects.
GCS requests originating in Europe experienced a 17% error rate.

errors when viewing clusters on the dashboard

The rate of errors is decreasing. We are investigating loss for traffic to and from South America. We are investigating loss of traffic with Google Networking in South America.

Google Cloud Storage service returning elevated error rates for requests in the US multi-region. Affected Google Cloud Functions customers may seen their deployments time out. Affected customers of Google Cloud Build were observable as "Build failed (internal error)"
approximately 60% of the Cloud SQL admin operations globally, including instance creation, are affected by the recent Cloud PubSub outage

service_name: Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router
user_symptom: We are experiencing an issue with packet loss for Google Cloud VPN, Cloud Interconnect, and Cloud Router beginning at Monday, 2018-10-22 13:42 US/Pacific. Current data indicate that requests in the following regions are affected by this issue: asia-east1, asia-south1, asia-southeast1, europe-west1, europe-west4, northamerica-northeast1, us-east1.

Google Compute Engine, Google Cloud VPN, and Google Cloud Network Load Balancers hosted in the us-east4 region experienced 100% packet loss from the internet and other GCP regions. Google Dedicated Interconnect Attachments located in us-east4 also experienced loss of connectivity.
Google Kubernetes Engine may clear certain add-ons from the configuration UI after a cluster upgrade.
Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely.
Issue affecting the ability to list projects and organizations
preventing the creation of new GCLB load balancers and updating of existing ones

Customers affected by this issue may see an error similar to the following:

ERROR: (gcloud.functions.deploy) OperationError: code=3, message=Build failed: # github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/extensions

src/github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/extensions/distributed_tracing_extension.go:161:3: cannot use span (type trace.Span) as type *trace.Span in return argument:

*trace.Span is pointer to interface, not interface; Error ID: 1093f764

We are experiencing an issue with Cloud Router monitoring data (metrics) missing beginning at Monday, 2020-12-14 12:00 US/Pacific. Routers themselves are not impacted and should be working with no issues. Visible decrease in Cloud Router data in Metrics Explorer.
All the components that require access to storage infrastructure may be affected by this issue in multiple regions.
Autoscaling of instances in Managed Instance Groups may not occur due to instances being unreachable.
Jobs using Dataflow Shuffle may fail or (when using Flexible Resource Scheduling) be queued indefinitely.
Customers are not able to create new databases in Cloud Firestore.
Customers peering with us-west2 may experience packet loss.
Google BigQuery job failures returning an "Internal Error" message.
We experiencing delays in showing logging.
increased system lag in some Google Cloud Dataflow jobs
Affected customers may experience an elevated error rate (up to 100%) when submitting dataset insertion operations.
users experiencing latency and high error rates
Customers may experience increased latency or connection timeouts between GCP and other Cloud Providers.
Affected customers may experience timeout errors upon cluster creation and deletion.
We are experiencing an intermittent issue with Cloud Firestore
We are investigating errors with Firebase Test Lab. We will be pausing all test execution until root cause is found and mitigated.
Cloud SQL instances in europe-west2-a may be unreachable
We are investigating an issue with Google Kubernetes Engine node pool creation through Cloud Console UI.
reduced URLFetch availability
Customers may observe their calico node pods failing to start and crash-looping indefinitely on GKE 1.24.7+ and 1.25.3+.
Affected customers were unable to create new instances, as the region field in the user interface (UI) could not be populated due to a metadata issue.
Cloud Functions Gen 2 customers are missing metrics and experiencing incorrect billing for their SKUs.

Google Compute Engine customers running “yum” or “dnf” commands in Red Hat instances experienced 403 forbidden responses when attempting to install packages. 100 % of customers running “yum” in RHEL instances received a 403 - Forbidden response. All Red Hat instances attempting to get updates encountered failures for the duration of the outage.
MemoryStore for Redis - instances update/export failures
Customers may have observed packet loss for traffic routed via transpacific links on Google's backbone.
ssh fails with ERROR: (gcloud.compute.start-iap-tunnel) Error while connecting [4047: 'Failed to lookup instance']
Calico enabled GKE clusters’ pods may get stuck Terminating or Pending after upgrading to 1.22+
Intermittent failures (ERROR: PERMISSION_DENIED: The caller does not have permission) when trying to list/describe the OAuth client via gCloud or Terraform
Customers may see failures while attempting to update/export their basic tier instances.

Affected customers may have experienced:
- All pre-existing Vertex models undeployed on Vertex AI Endpoints
- Empty responses when listing the deployed models
- Runtime exceptions and general errors on Predict and Explain requests
- Quota failure when trying to re-deploy models
Affected customers would have experienced elevated query latencies or in some cases query failures.

Customers may have been unable to create or edit Google Compute Engine (GCE) instances via the Google Cloud Console may have received an error “Constraint constraints/compute.vmExternalIpAccess violated for project [project ID].“

Customers were unable to access their Cloud Volume Service (CVS) storage across GCP Regions.
Customers may have faced issues with traffic routing In some cases where dynamic routes were not propagated appropriately on peered networks across regions.

External HTTP/S Load Balancing, Cloud CDN - config changes will be accepted & fail to propagate
Cloud Armor rules may also be affected
Appengine flex customers may see apps fail to deploy
Traffic Director may see configs fail to propagate
VM instance group healthchecks & functionality like autoscaling/autohealing may be affected

Customer may experience replication failure on the new CSA rollout on replica, if the master instance is busy with INSERTs/UPDATEs on tables with timestamp column and session timezone is set to other than SYSTEM.

Affected customers would have experienced increased packet loss and increased latency for the affected metros which would have caused delays and timeouts for the following services : - Cloud Load Balancing - Cloud Interconnect - Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) - Cloud NAT - Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
Customers may observe deployment failures and timeout errors.
Customers will see INTERNAL_ERROR when performing ClusterCreation (GKE Cluster Server receives invalid_argument error when creating a GCS bucket as one step of cluster creation).
Customers might have experienced increased errors from apps or functions that use the Secrets Manager.
Cloud Composer v2 environments autoscaling might not work. Number of Airflow workers in the Composer environment won’t change.

Customer configs propagation depending on the dos_cloud configs got delayed.
Some impacted customers would not be able to make Cloud Armor config changes.

Error message:
Operation Failed. Public Error Status: code: 5 message: Delete package operation for projects/[PROJECT_ID]/locations/[REGION]/repositories/gcf-artifacts/packages/[FUNCTION_ID]%2Fcustom-run has failed. Additional information: Requested entity was not found.
Cloud SQL for SQL Server is seeing a spike in instance creation failures globally.
Customers might have experienced a cloud networking disruption from 04:30 AM - 04:58 AM US/Pacific as a result of an issue on physical network.

Affected customers would not have been able to create new repositories in the Asia multi-region. Customers would have received a failure message of “Request contains an invalid argument.”
- Affected customers in the Asia multi-region were not able to create Composer environments, receiving errors indicating either an issue with AppEngine or Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster creation.
- Affected customers would have experienced increased error rates when creating buckets in the Asia multi-region.
- Affected customers would have been unable to create new clusters in the affected regions.

As a result of this throttling, read-only API access was unavailable for all customers. Console, gcloud and API access (List, GetOperation, etc.) calls all failed for a duration of 3 hours, 12 minutes.
Customers may experience latency and errors with Composer creation.

Our engineers have determined that Google Cloud Networking was impacted by the same underlying issue as the Google Compute Engine (GCE) incident. The start & end times have been updated in this incident to reflect the approximate impact period. Please refer to [https://status.cloud.google.com/incident/compute/19008](https://status.cloud.google.com/incident/compute/19008) for the full impact details of the incident.
